title: Revised Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) Guidelines
description: The RBI released updated guidelines for the Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR), introducing key changes that take effect from April 1, 2026, aimed at strengthening the liquidity resilience of Indian banks and bringing domestic regulations in line with global standards. Banks must assign an additional run-off rate of 2.5% to deposits made through internet and mobile banking channels by retail and small business customers, and apply haircuts to Government Securities classified as Level 1 High-Quality Liquid Assets in accordance with LAF and MSF requirements.
